themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, where President Trump was present, has become a dominant cross-national story in right-wing media. Coverage spans multiple countries, with leaders and media outlets condemning the violence and expressing solidarity with Trump. The incident is framed as an attack on democracy and free speech, resonating strongly across borders.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Intense coverage of the shooting, Trump’s evacuation, and global reactions, with emphasis on security failures and political violence.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni and other Italian leaders condemned the attack, framing it as an assault on democratic values and expressing solidarity with Trump.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: French far-right media highlighted the incident as evidence of rising political violence and instability in the U.S.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Coverage focused on the security breach and broader implications for political discourse, with calls for unity against extremism.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Spanish right-wing media framed the shooting as part of a broader pattern of political violence, criticizing left-wing rhetoric as a contributing factor.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: German far-right media used the incident to criticize U.S. political polarization and advocate for stronger security measures.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish right-wing media emphasized the attack as a threat to democratic norms and praised Trump’s resilience.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a surge in polling numbers, with the AfD in Germany leading national polls for the first time. This trend reflects growing voter dissatisfaction with mainstream parties, particularly on issues like immigration, economic policy, and national sovereignty. The coverage highlights parallel electoral successes and the potential for far-right influence in upcoming elections.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads national polls at 28%, surpassing CDU/CSU, amid rising voter dissatisfaction with mainstream parties.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN prepares for the 2027 French presidential election, with Jordan Bardella unveiling economic policies aimed at appealing to working-class voters.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox gains momentum in regional elections, particularly in Andalusia, by focusing on immigration and national priority policies.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sweden Democrats influence government policies, particularly on immigration and energy, despite internal coalition tensions.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Norwegian Progress Party gains traction by advocating for stricter immigration policies and economic deregulation.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: Coalition agreements between Spain’s Partido Popular (PP) and Vox in regions like Extremadura and Aragón have ignited cross-national debates over constitutional principles, particularly regarding immigration and national priority policies. These pacts are criticized for potentially violating non-discrimination clauses in Spain’s constitution and have drawn sharp reactions from opposition parties and legal experts.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s ‘national priority’ policy in coalition agreements with PP sparks legal and ethical debates over discrimination and constitutional compliance.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP’s alliances with Vox in Extremadura and Aragón draw criticism for adopting far-right policies, particularly on immigration and social welfare.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 PSOE
countries.note: Socialist Party condemns PP-Vox pacts as unconstitutional and discriminatory, using them to rally opposition support.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: French far-right media highlights PP-Vox coalitions as evidence of Europe’s rightward shift, advocating for similar policies in France.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing media critiques the coalitions for undermining democratic values and social cohesion, drawing parallels to Italy’s own political dynamics.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán’s decision to resign his parliamentary seat following Fidesz’s electoral defeat has sent shockwaves through Hungarian politics. The move is seen as a strategic retreat to reorganize the party and maintain influence behind the scenes. Cross-national coverage highlights Orbán’s enduring role in shaping Hungary’s political landscape and the rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party as a new force.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán’s resignation and Fidesz’s electoral defeat dominate coverage, with debates over the party’s future and Magyar’s rise.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish right-wing media analyzes Orbán’s resignation as a cautionary tale for PiS, emphasizing the need for party renewal.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: German far-right media views Orbán’s resignation as a sign of Europe’s shifting political landscape, with potential implications for AfD.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU media focuses on the potential impact of Orbán’s resignation on Hungary’s access to EU funds and rule-of-law negotiations.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right media across Europe and the U.S. are amplifying rhetoric that is increasingly linked to political violence. Incidents such as the shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner and vandalism of far-right party offices are framed as consequences of inflammatory discourse. This theme highlights the transnational spread of divisive political messaging and its real-world impact.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing media condemns vandalism of Lega offices, framing it as left-wing extremism and political intolerance.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Spanish far-right media highlights attacks on Vox events and offices, blaming left-wing groups for escalating political violence.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: British right-wing media focuses on alleged sabotage of Reform UK campaign materials, framing it as an attack on democracy.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: U.S. right-wing media amplifies rhetoric linking political violence to left-wing extremism, particularly in the wake of the WHCD shooting.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: French far-right media criticizes left-wing protests and vandalism, framing them as threats to democratic values.
